query: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd
results:
Title: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd

Description:
﻿### Problem

VS Code injects `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at an early position in the integrated terminal's process PATH. This causes `code` to resolve to `Code.exe` (the GUI binary) instead of `code.cmd` in the `bin\` subfolder.

Title: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd

Description:
﻿### Problem

VS Code injects `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at an early position in the integrated terminal's process PATH. This causes `code` to resolve to `Code.exe` (the GUI binary) instead of `code.cmd` in the `bin\` subfolder.

### Expected Behavior

`code` should resolve to `code.cmd` in `bin\`, which sets ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 and runs cli.js correctly.

### Root Cause

VS Code prepends its installation directory (containing Code.exe) to the terminal's process PATH, shadowing the correct `bin\code.cmd`.

### Impact on Copilot Agent Mode

query: OutputMonitor.continueMonitoringAsync does not cancel the previous monitoring run before starting a new one
results:
Title: OutputMonitor.continueMonitoringAsync does not cancel the previous monitoring run before starting a new one

Description:
[`OutputMonitor.continueMonitoringAsync`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/1dfb3b9dd41665724360e94791708dfcab81b681/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/monitoring/outputMonitor.ts#L209) starts a new monitoring run without stopping the previous one:

The foreground→background handoff in `runInTerminalTool` calls `continueMonitoringAsync` while the original monitoring pass is still running. With the cancellation bug above, that path can have two monitoring runs alive at once. `OutputMonitor` keeps per-run state on the instance, so both runs share `this._state`, `this._pollingResult`, `this._userInputListener`, and the emitters.

The `UnresponsiveSampleError` is caused by **catastrophic regex backtracking** (ReDoS) in the `detectsInputRequiredPattern()` function in `outputMonitor.ts`. When the terminal output monitor polls for input-required patterns, the PowerShell prompt detection regex (line 472) exhibits O(n2) backtracking on terminal output containing a `[X]`-style prefix followed by a long sequence of whitespace mixed with non-whitespace characters. This freezes the renderer process, triggering VS Code's

query: OS toast notification shows wrong terminal command when multiple tool invocations in response
results:
Title: OS toast notification shows wrong terminal command when multiple tool invocations in response

Description:
## Bug

When a chat response contains multiple terminal tool invocations, the OS notification toast shows the command from the **first** terminal invocation (which may have already completed) rather than the one actually **waiting for confirmation**.

### Repro

### Repro

1. Start a session where the agent runs multiple terminal commands sequentially
2. The first command (e.g. `git status --short`) completes and the agent moves on
3. A later command (e.g. a complex `npx playwright test ...` invocation) requires user confirmation
4. The OS toast notification fires but displays the first command (`git status --short`) instead of the pending one

### Cause

**Fix location 2**: `runInTerminalTool.ts` → `_registerCompletionNotification()`  
**Approach**: Change `_backgroundNotifications` key from `termId` (unique per invocation) to `terminalInstance.instanceId` (unique per terminal). This way, re-registering completion notifications for the same terminal properly disposes the old listeners via `deleteAndDispose`. The `termId` can still be stored inside the `DisposableStore` for use in the notification callbacks.

### Recommended Owner

Title: `send_to_terminal` auto approval handling improvements

Description:
The `send_to_terminal` tool is almost exclusively invoked after the `ask_questions` tool has been used to seek user's confirmation. In that case, the command has already been approved, so doesn't need to go through the usual auto approval rules. 

However, if it gets invoked directly (not via ask questions), auto approval rules are not applied, but should be.



Comments:
I had yolo mode enabled

query: why is copilot searching the wrong file
results: Title: Copilot diff is broken and unreadable on a single file.

Need to check if this is a regression in CLI or not.
Or perhaps has always been an issue, and possible CLI only works with `copilot-instructions.md` file
Copilot CLI Docs are not necessarily upto date with how CLI works

### What changed
Commit [microsoft/vscode-copilot-chat@7dc4b8e](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-copilot-chat/commit/7dc4b8e) ("tools: avoid putting fences into file reads by default", 2026-02-06) changed the read_file tool output in two ways:

- Removed code fences (triple backticks wrapping file content)
- Removed the metadata header line that previously read: 
```
File: `path/to/file`. Lines 1 to 200 (430 lines total):
```

Old output (working, pre-1.108):

query: VS Code terminal PATH causing issues
results:
Title: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd

Description:
﻿### Problem

VS Code injects `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at an early position in the integrated terminal's process PATH. This causes `code` to resolve to `Code.exe` (the GUI binary) instead of `code.cmd` in the `bin\` subfolder.

**The linked issues are different:**
- #300231, #303021, #302026 are about elevated/admin terminals — I'm running as a normal user, no elevation
- This is purely about VS Code prepending the installation **root** directory (which contains `Code.exe`) to the terminal's process PATH, shadowing the correct `bin\code.cmd` from the system PATH

The `UnresponsiveSampleError` is caused by **catastrophic regex backtracking** (ReDoS) in the `detectsInputRequiredPattern()` function in `outputMonitor.ts`. When the terminal output monitor polls for input-required patterns, the PowerShell prompt detection regex (line 472) exhibits O(n2) backtracking on terminal output containing a `[X]`-style prefix followed by a long sequence of whitespace mixed with non-whitespace characters. This freezes the renderer process, triggering VS Code's

When a GitHub MCP `get_file_contents` tool call returns a large file (50KB–999KB, under the MCP server's 1MB hard limit), VS Code's context window manager truncates the tool result before it reaches the LLM and marks the response `isTruncated=true`.

query: OS notification for terminal commands is wrong
results:
Title: OS toast notification shows wrong terminal command when multiple tool invocations in response

Description:
## Bug

When a chat response contains multiple terminal tool invocations, the OS notification toast shows the command from the **first** terminal invocation (which may have already completed) rather than the one actually **waiting for confirmation**.

### Repro

### Repro

1. Start a session where the agent runs multiple terminal commands sequentially
2. The first command (e.g. `git status --short`) completes and the agent moves on
3. A later command (e.g. a complex `npx playwright test ...` invocation) requires user confirmation
4. The OS toast notification fires but displays the first command (`git status --short`) instead of the pending one

### Cause

Title: `chat.tools.terminal.backgroundNotifications: false` still fires completion notifications for foreground terminals

Description:
Testing #309095 (section 1, Windows).

Title: `send_to_terminal` progress message repeats first question when accepting all defaults in interactive commands

Description:
## Bug

When an interactive command like `npm init` is run and the user accepts all defaults via the `askQuestions` carousel, every `send_to_terminal` progress message displays the first question's text (e.g. "Package name?") instead of the correct question for each prompt.

## Steps to Reproduce

Comments:
Sharing media from merged PR #309050 for verification:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7ad84afc-4720-47af-ad3a-0809af0a63dd

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3bfa38c-b115-4af5-bb88-09e00e163637

Proposed Improvement:
Selecting a session tab should automatically focus the corresponding terminal pane, allowing immediate interaction with the shell using a single click.

This change would streamline the user experience and make session switching faster and more intuitive, especially for users managing multiple concurrent terminal sessions.

Comments:
you can do this with `terminal.integrated.tabs.focusMode`

A **"Focus terminal"** button is displayed alongside this message. This behavior is confusing and disrupts the workflow, as it suggests user action is needed when it is not.

Sometimes when I don't do nothing, the message disappeares on its own and the chat continues.
Sometimes I need to press on "Focus terminal", where I see a finished successful shell execution. In that terminal I press Enter out of confusion - and chat continues.

query: how to add focus terminal to ask questions
results:
Title: add `focus terminal` option to `ask_questions` tool when invoked from terminal tool

Description:
In case a user wants to type their password directly for example

Comments:
Sharing media from merged PR #309050 for verification:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7ad84afc-4720-47af-ad3a-0809af0a63dd

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3bfa38c-b115-4af5-bb88-09e00e163637

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/89892609-65c3-4394-92ec-d58682d03ce6

**Key changes:**
- Removed LLM-based prompt-for-input detection (saves tokens + latency per command)
- `send_to_terminal` and `get_terminal_output` now work with foreground terminals, not just background
- `ask_questions` carousel gains a "Focus Terminal" button when triggered by terminal prompts
- Carousel auto-dismisses when user types directly in terminal
- `send_to_terminal` progress messages show the question being answered
- Confirmation dialog uses terminal title instead of raw UUID

A **"Focus terminal"** button is displayed alongside this message. This behavior is confusing and disrupts the workflow, as it suggests user action is needed when it is not.

Sometimes when I don't do nothing, the message disappeares on its own and the chat continues.
Sometimes I need to press on "Focus terminal", where I see a finished successful shell execution. In that terminal I press Enter out of confusion - and chat continues.
